existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Hand & Wrist Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite forearm-to-fingertip model linking radius, ulna, carpus, metacarpals, digits, tendons, ligaments, nerves, and vessels for Prepare Hand rotation.
Lateral forearm bone forming the radial side of the wrist and radiocarpal articulation.
Medial forearm bone with distal ulnar head and styloid contributing to DRUJ stability.
Forearm longitudinal fibrous linkage transmitting axial load between radius and ulna.
Distal radioulnar joint stabilizers and ulnar-sided wrist linkage.
Proximal radioulnar joint enabling forearm rotation.
Distal radial metaphysis and articular surface at the wrist.
Ulnar head and styloid region at the ulnar wrist.
Radial-sided carpal bone with retrograde blood supply and snuffbox tenderness landmark.
Central proximal carpal bone in perilunate and lunate dislocation patterns.
Ulnar-sided proximal carpal bone articulating with the lunate and TFCC.
Sesamoid bone within FCU tendon at the ulnar wrist.
Radial-sided distal carpal bone articulating with the thumb metacarpal.
Distal carpal bone between trapezium and capitate.
Central distal carpal bone linking proximal and distal carpal rows.
Ulnar-sided distal carpal bone with hook for flexor tendon passage.
Thumb metacarpal articulating with trapezium at the CMC joint.
Index metacarpal with stable CMC articulation.
Middle finger metacarpal forming the central hand column.
Ring finger metacarpal.
Small finger metacarpal; common site of boxer fractures.
Proximal segment of each digit articulating at MCP and PIP.
Middle segment of digits 2–5 between PIP and DIP.
Terminal phalangeal segment bearing the nail bed.
Distal interphalangeal articulation of digits 2–5 and IP of the thumb.
Proximal interphalangeal articulation governing finger flexion cascade.
Metacarpophalangeal articulation linking metacarpal to proximal phalanx.
Carpometacarpal articulation; thumb CMC is saddle-shaped and highly mobile.
Intrinsic ligament between scaphoid and lunate; SL injury drives carpal instability.
Intrinsic ligament between lunate and triquetrum; LT injury contributes to ulnar-sided instability.
Triangular fibrocartilage complex stabilizing the ulnar wrist and DRUJ.
Ulnar collateral ligament of the thumb MCP joint; injured in skier/gamekeeper thumb.
Fibrocartilaginous volar restraint at PIP preventing hyperextension.
Radial and ulnar collateral ligaments stabilizing MCP and IP joints.
Flexor pollicis longus tendon flexing the thumb IP joint.
Flexor digitorum profundus tendons flexing DIP joints; zone II no-man's land.
Flexor digitorum superficialis tendons flexing PIP joints.
Extensor pollicis longus tendon extending the thumb IP joint.
Extensor pollicis brevis tendon extending the thumb MCP joint.
Abductor pollicis longus tendon abducting and extending the thumb.
Extensor digitorum communis tendons extending MCP and IP joints.
Extensor carpi ulnaris tendon depressor and ulnar deviator at the wrist.
Flexor carpi radialis tendon; volar wrist approach interval landmark.
Flexor carpi ulnaris tendon inserting on pisiform and stabilizing ulnar wrist.
Flexor tendon pulley A1 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A2 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A3 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A4 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley A5 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C1 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C2 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Flexor tendon pulley C3 Pulley restraining flexor tendons against the phalanges.
Annular (A1–A5) and cruciate (C1–C3) pulleys preventing bowstringing of flexor tendons.
Zone I–V flexor tendon anatomic divisions governing repair strategy and prognosis.
Zone I–VIII extensor tendon anatomic divisions governing repair and splinting strategy.
Median nerve at risk in carpal tunnel and volar wrist surgery.
Ulnar nerve at Guyon canal and cubital tunnel; digital branches in the hand.
Radial nerve and branches at the wrist and dorsum of the hand.
Anterior interosseous nerve branch of median nerve; motor to FPL and index FDP.
Posterior interosseous nerve branch of radial nerve at the proximal forearm.
Proper digital nerves supplying sensibility to the fingers.
Radial artery at the wrist forming part of the deep arch.
Ulnar artery at Guyon canal contributing to palmar arches.
Palmar arterial arcade primarily from ulnar artery.
Deep palmar arterial arcade primarily from radial artery.
Osteofibrous canal containing median nerve and flexor tendons beneath the transverse carpal ligament.
Ulnar tunnel at the wrist containing ulnar nerve and artery.
Prepare Hand rotation neighborhood for Carpal Tunnel Syndrome — Tendons & Nerves.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Recurrent Motor Branch of the Median Nerve

metadata.description: Median nerve motor branch relevant to thenar function and carpal tunnel release.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Palmar Cutaneous Branch of the Median Nerve
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Palmar Cutaneous Branch of the Median Nerve

metadata.description: Median nerve sensory branch relevant to localization and operative exposure.
Thenar muscle used in focused median motor examination and electrodiagnostic assessment.
Provocative wrist-flexion maneuver used as one component of CTS assessment.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Tinel Sign at the Carpal Tunnel
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Tinel Sign at the Carpal Tunnel

metadata.description: Median nerve percussion maneuver used as one component of CTS assessment.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Durkan Carpal Compression Test

metadata.description: Direct carpal tunnel compression maneuver used as one component of CTS assessment.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Electrodiagnostic Testing for Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Electrodiagnostic Testing for Carpal Tunnel Syndrome

metadata.description: Combined electrodiagnostic evaluation used selectively for CTS diagnosis, localization, and severity assessment.
Electrodiagnostic test measuring peripheral nerve conduction.
Needle examination of muscle electrical activity used as a component of electrodiagnostic evaluation.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Neutral Wrist Night Orthosis for CTS

metadata.description: CTS-specific nonoperative night orthosis principle, distinct from fracture immobilization.

metadata.description: Local corticosteroid injection used for selected patients with CTS.

metadata.description: Open operative decompression procedure for CTS.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Endoscopic Carpal Tunnel Release

metadata.description: Endoscopic operative decompression procedure for CTS.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Open Carpal Tunnel Approach

metadata.description: Open palmar exposure for carpal tunnel release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Endoscopic Carpal Tunnel Approach

metadata.description: Endoscopic portal exposure for carpal tunnel release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Carpal Tunnel Release Instrument Set

metadata.description: Technique-dependent instrument set for open or endoscopic carpal tunnel release.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Pillar Pain After Carpal Tunnel Release
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Pillar Pain After Carpal Tunnel Release

metadata.description: Postoperative pain localized around the carpal tunnel pillars after release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Incomplete Carpal Tunnel Release

metadata.description: Incomplete operative decompression considered during evaluation of persistent symptoms.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Persistent Carpal Tunnel Syndrome After Release
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Persistent Carpal Tunnel Syndrome After Release

metadata.description: CTS symptoms that do not adequately improve after release; distinct from recurrence after improvement.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Recurrent Carpal Tunnel Syndrome

metadata.description: Return of CTS symptoms after a period of improvement following release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Revision Carpal Tunnel Release

metadata.description: Revision decompression procedure considered after source-supported evaluation of failed release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Carpal Tunnel Release Postoperative Protocol

metadata.description: Individualized postoperative care and rehabilitation framework after carpal tunnel release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; source before manufacture

metadata.claim_text: Carpal tunnel syndrome evaluation combines the symptom pattern, focused median nerve examination, and selective diagnostic testing rather than relying on routine imaging.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending anatomy review required

metadata.claim_text: Carpal tunnel syndrome localizes to the median nerve within the carpal tunnel; release planning also requires review of median nerve branch anatomy.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; source before manufacture

metadata.claim_text: Document sensory distribution, thenar motor findings, and provocative maneuvers while considering proximal median neuropathy, cervical radiculopathy, and other mimics.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; EDX specialist review required

metadata.claim_text: Electrodiagnostic testing is a selective diagnostic and severity tool; MRI and repeat radiographs are not routine CTS follow-up tests.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.claim_text: CTS treatment planning distinguishes neutral-position night splinting and corticosteroid injection from named open and endoscopic release procedures.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.claim_text: Persistent symptoms, recurrent symptoms, incomplete release, pillar pain, and iatrogenic nerve injury are distinct concepts and should not be collapsed into a generic complication.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending and rehabilitation review required

metadata.claim_text: Post-release activity, therapy, and return-to-work guidance should be individualized; routine fracture-style immobilization and repeat radiographs do not apply.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; educator and domain review required

metadata.claim_text: OITE content should distinguish palmar cutaneous and recurrent motor branch anatomy, selective test use, and persistent symptoms from recurrent disease and incomplete release.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; EDX specialist review required

metadata.trigger: Atypical presentation, uncertain localization or severity, competing diagnosis, or a result expected to change management
metadata.action: Consider targeted electrodiagnostic or other supported testing; do not default to routine radiographs or MRI

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: Presentation appropriate for an initial nonoperative pathway without an attending-reviewed escalation indication
metadata.action: Discuss education, neutral-position night splinting, and/or corticosteroid injection with source-specific expectations and follow-up

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: Persistent function-limiting symptoms after appropriate care, severe disease, or objective motor or denervation findings
metadata.action: Discuss a named carpal tunnel release option after confirming diagnosis, severity, patient factors, and goals

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: A reviewed surgical indication is present and open versus endoscopic technique is being selected
metadata.action: Use shared decision-making informed by anatomy, prior surgery, risks, resources, patient preference, and surgeon expertise

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: Inadequate visualization, anomalous anatomy, bleeding, or another safety concern during endoscopic release
metadata.action: Stop or convert according to an attending-approved bailout protocol

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: Symptoms do not adequately improve after carpal tunnel release
metadata.action: Reassess diagnosis, localization, severity, and release completeness before considering revision

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

metadata.trigger: CTS symptoms return after a period of improvement following release
metadata.action: Evaluate recurrent compression and alternative causes before considering revision

existingProposals.evidence_summary: CTS vertical preparation; attending and rehabilitation review required

metadata.trigger: Progressive neurologic deficit, vascular concern, wound concern, or severe unexpected postoperative pain
metadata.action: Obtain prompt surgical assessment under the treating team's protocol
